spans.text: the grey cope , and the hollowness Of heaven
, far off , descry , That I might weep tears of delight , and say
On some rude shore , Around whose crags the cheerless billows roar , Watching the unwearied surges
the sound of these encircling seas
think I listen to his echoing shell
I listen to his echoing shell
it spread its white wings to the wind , Too soon it left these hills and woods behind , Gazing
left these hills and woods behind
was seen no more , And only the blue hollow cope I spied , And the long waste of waters tossing wide
no more , And only the blue hollow cope I spied , And the long waste of waters tossing wide
the stagnant tear upon my beard
amid their roar At midnight , Thou shalt see thy son no more
the high wood loud , Over all the beach
